name: Christmas Casserole
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Drain salmon in a large bowl.
3. Remove skin and bones from the salmon; flake into small pieces.
4. Coarsely crush soda crackers with a rolling pin.
5. Add crushed crackers to the salmon, reserving about 2 tablespoons for topping.
6. Stir in corn, cheese, milk, green pepper, and pepper.
7. Beat egg lightly in a small bowl, then add it to the salmon-corn mixture.
8. Mix well until combined.
9. Transfer the mixture to a greased 6-cup casserole dish with a lid.
10. Smooth the top of the casserole.
11. Sprinkle reserved crushed crackers over the top and dot with butter.
12. Sprinkle sliced almonds on top.
13. Bake covered for 30 minutes at 425°F, then uncover for the last 5 minutes.
This is based on my grandmother's recipe and was originally made with oysters. She lived in Iowa and in those days (20s & 30s); oysters came in on the trains once a year at Christmas time. Unfortunately, living in Toronto, we don't get oysters on the trains! Serves: 4
